---
title: "Introduction to vigiar"
author: "Ryan Santos"
date: "2026-06-20"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Introduction to vigiar}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE
)
```

## Overview

`vigiar` downloads, processes, validates, and documents data from the public
VIGIAR Power BI dashboard maintained by Brazil's Ministry of Health. The package
focuses first on PM2.5 and the 92 municipalities of Rio de Janeiro state.

VIGIAR publishes data on:

- PM2.5 concentration by municipality and time period;
- days above WHO and CONAMA PM2.5 references;
- population exposure categories;
- aggregate health indicators and attributable fractions;
- indoor solid-fuel exposure indicators.

The package is designed for reproducible epidemiologic data work. It checks
schema assumptions, municipality codes, RJ coverage, possible API truncation,
and scientific limitations before analysis.

## Installation

```r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("santosry/vigiar")
```

## Connecting

```r
library(vigiar)

vigiar_conectar()
vigiar_tabelas()
vigiar_esquema("df_anual")
```

The connection reads public Power BI metadata and does not require login.

## Downloading RJ Data

Use the RJ-specific downloader when the intended scope is Rio de Janeiro:

```r
pm25_rj <- vigiar_baixar_rj("df_anual", validar_cobertura = TRUE)
vigiar_rj_cobertura(pm25_rj)
vigiar_rj_municipios_ausentes(pm25_rj)
```

When all 92 municipalities are required:

```r
pm25_rj <- vigiar_baixar_rj(
  "df_anual",
  validar_cobertura = TRUE,
  exigir_completo = TRUE
)
```

`exigir_completo = TRUE` fails loudly if any expected RJ municipality is absent.
This is useful for research workflows that require a complete municipal panel.

## Processing

```r
pm25 <- process_pm25(pm25_rj, tipo = "anual")

diag <- vigiar_diagnosticar_serie(pm25, escopo = "rj")
vigiar_relatorio_diagnostico(diag)
```

The PM2.5 processor standardizes column names, normalizes municipality codes to
the 6-digit IBGE standard, validates dates and units, and returns a typed
`vigiar_pm25` tibble.

## Responsible Use

VIGIAR data are public and aggregated. PM2.5 may be estimated or modelled, and
coverage can vary by table, year, month, and source availability. A dataset can
be available without being complete for RJ.

Always inspect coverage before making municipality, macro-region, or temporal
claims.

## Closing the Session

```r
vigiar_desconectar()
```
